
# Git Workflow for Forking and Syncing with Upstream (SSH)

This document explains how to convert an existing local clone into a proper fork setup using SSH, while keeping the **master** branch in sync with the original repository.

## Diagram
Below is the full workflow diagram embedded directly in this file:

![Git Workflow Diagram](data:image/png;base64,)

---

# 1. Fork the Repository on GitHub
Original repository:
https://github.com/kspaceKelvin/python-ismrmrd-server

Create your fork under your account:
https://github.com/RalfLoeffler

Your fork SSH URL:
```
git@github.com:RalfLoeffler/python-ismrmrd-server.git
```

---

# 2. Point Your Existing Local Clone to Your Fork
If you cloned the original repo first, your `origin` still points there.
Change it to your fork:
```
git remote set-url origin git@github.com:RalfLoeffler/python-ismrmrd-server.git
```

---

# 3. Add the Original Repository as `upstream`
This lets you receive updates from the original project.
```
git remote add upstream git@github.com:kspaceKelvin/python-ismrmrd-server.git
```

---

# 4. Verify the Remote Setup
```
git remote -v
```
Expected output:
```
origin    git@github.com:RalfLoeffler/python-ismrmrd-server.git (fetch)
origin    git@github.com:RalfLoeffler/python-ismrmrd-server.git (push)
upstream  git@github.com:kspaceKelvin/python-ismrmrd-server.git (fetch)
upstream  git@github.com:kspaceKelvin/python-ismrmrd-server.git (push)
```

---

# 5. Sync Your Local `master` with the Original Repository
Fetch latest changes:
```
git fetch upstream
```

Merge into your local master:
```
git checkout master
git merge upstream/master
```

Or rebase instead:
```
git checkout master
git rebase upstream/master
```

Push updated master to your fork:
```
git push origin master
```

---

# 6. Typical Workflow Summary
- Work on feature branches in **your fork** (`origin`)
- Periodically sync from **upstream**
- Push updates back to your fork
- (Optional) Open pull requests to contribute back upstream

---

# 7. Quick Command Reference
```
# point origin to your SSH fork
git remote set-url origin git@github.com:RalfLoeffler/python-ismrmrd-server.git

# add upstream
git remote add upstream git@github.com:kspaceKelvin/python-ismrmrd-server.git

# update master
git fetch upstream
git checkout master
git merge upstream/master
# or: git rebase upstream/master

git push origin master
```
